NKJV.1: When Jesus had spoken these words, He went out with His disciples over the Brook Kidron, where there was a garden, which He and His disciples entered.
NKJV.2: And Judas, who betrayed Him, also knew the place; for Jesus often met there with His disciples.
NKJV.3: Then Judas, having received a detachment of troops, and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.
NKJV.4: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that would come upon Him, went forward and said to them, “Whom are you seeking?”
NKJV.5: They answered Him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am He.” And Judas, who betrayed Him, also stood with them.
NKJV.6: Now when He said to them, “I am He,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
NKJV.7: Then He asked them again, “Whom are you seeking?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
NKJV.8: Jesus answered, “I have told you that I am He. Therefore, if you seek Me, let these go their way,”
NKJV.9: that the saying might be fulfilled which He spoke, “Of those whom You gave Me I have lost none.”
NKJV.10: Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
NKJV.11: So Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword into the sheath. Shall I not drink  the cup which My Father has given Me?”
NKJV.12: Then the detachment of troops and the captain and the officers of the Jews arrested Jesus and bound Him.
NKJV.13: And they led Him away to Annas first, for he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas who was high priest that year.
NKJV.14: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
NKJV.15: And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.
NKJV.16: But Peter stood at the door outside. Then the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought Peter in.
NKJV.17: Then the servant girl who kept the door said to Peter, “You are not also one of this Man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
NKJV.18: Now the servants and officers who had made a fire of coals stood there, for it was cold, and they warmed themselves. And Peter stood with them and warmed himself.
NKJV.19: The high priest then asked Jesus about His disciples and His doctrine.
NKJV.20: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught  in synagogues and  in the temple, where the Jews always meet, and in secret I have said nothing.
NKJV.21: Why do you ask Me? Ask  those who have heard Me what I said to them. Indeed they know what I said.”
NKJV.22: And when He had said these things, one of the officers who stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, “Do You answer the high priest like that?”
NKJV.23: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil; but if well, why do you strike Me?”
NKJV.24: Then Annas sent Him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
NKJV.25: Now Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. Therefore they said to him, “You are not also one of His disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not!”
NKJV.26: One of the servants of the high priest, a relative of him whose ear Peter cut off, said, “Did I not see you in the garden with Him?”
NKJV.27: Peter then denied again; and immediately a rooster crowed.
NKJV.28: Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Praetorium, and it was early morning. But they themselves did not go into the Praetorium, lest they should be defiled, but that they might eat the Passover.
NKJV.29: Pilate then went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this Man?”
NKJV.30: They answered and said to him, “If He were not an evildoer, we would not have delivered Him up to you.”
NKJV.31: Then Pilate said to them, “You take Him and judge Him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,”
NKJV.32: that the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled which He spoke, signifying by what death He would die.
NKJV.33: Then Pilate entered the Praetorium again, called Jesus, and said to Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”
NKJV.34: Jesus answered him, “Are you speaking for yourself about this, or did others tell you this concerning Me?”
NKJV.35: Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered You to me. What have You done?”
NKJV.36: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, My servants would fight, so that I should not be delivered to the Jews; but now My kingdom is not from here.”
NKJV.37: Pilate therefore said to Him, “Are You a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say rightly that I am a king. For this cause I was born, and for this cause I have come into the world, that I should bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”

ESV.1: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples across the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, which he and his disciples entered.
ESV.2: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples.
ESV.3: So Judas, having procured a band of soldiers and some officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, went there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
ESV.4: Then Jesus, knowing all that would happen to him, came forward and said to them,  “Whom do you seek?”
ESV.5: They answered him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am he.”  Judas, who betrayed him, was standing with them.
ESV.6: When Jesus  said to them, “I am he,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
ESV.7: So he asked them again,  “Whom do you seek?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
ESV.8: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. So, if you seek me, let these men go.”
ESV.9: This was to fulfill the word that he had spoken: “Of those whom you gave me I have lost not one.”
ESV.10: Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant  and cut off his right ear. (The servant's name was Malchus.)
ESV.11: So Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword into its sheath; shall I not drink the cup that the Father has given me?”
ESV.12: So the band of soldiers and their captain and the officers of the Jews  arrested Jesus and bound him.
ESV.13: First they led him to Annas, for he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
ESV.14: It was Caiaphas who had advised the Jews that it would be expedient that one man should die for the people.
ESV.15: Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple. Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he entered with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest,
ESV.16: but Peter stood outside at the door. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the servant girl who kept watch at the door, and brought Peter in.
ESV.17: The servant girl at the door said to Peter, “You also are not one of this man's disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
ESV.18: Now the servants  and officers had made a charcoal fire, because it was cold, and they were standing and warming themselves. Peter also was with them, standing and warming himself.
ESV.19: The high priest then questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
ESV.20: Jesus answered him, “I have spoken openly to the world. I have always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all Jews come together. I have said nothing in secret.
ESV.21: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them; they know what I said.”
ESV.22: When he had said these things, one of the officers standing by struck Jesus with his hand, saying, “Is that how you answer the high priest?”
ESV.23: Jesus answered him, “If what I said is wrong, bear witness about the wrong; but if what I said is right, why do you strike me?”
ESV.24: Annas then sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
ESV.25: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. So they said to him, “You also are not one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”
ESV.26: One of the servants of the high priest, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Did I not see you in the garden with him?”
ESV.27: Peter again denied it, and at once a rooster crowed.
ESV.28: Then they led Jesus from the house of Caiaphas to the governor's headquarters.  It was early morning. They themselves did not enter the governor's headquarters, so that they would not be defiled, but could eat the Passover.
ESV.29: So Pilate went outside to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
ESV.30: They answered him, “If this man were not doing evil, we would not have delivered him over to you.”
ESV.31: Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and judge him by your own law.” The Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death.”
ESV.32: This was to fulfill the word that Jesus had spoken to show by what kind of death he was going to die.
ESV.33: So Pilate entered his headquarters again and called Jesus and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”
ESV.34: Jesus answered, “Do you say this of your own accord, or did others say it to you about me?”
ESV.35: Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered you over to me. What have you done?”
ESV.36: Jesus answered,  “My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have been fighting, that I might not be delivered over to the Jews. But my kingdom is not from the world.”
ESV.37: Then Pilate said to him, “So you are a king?” Jesus answered,  “You say that I am a king. For this purpose I was born and for this purpose I have come into the world — to bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”
ESV.38: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?” After he had said this, he went back outside to the Jews and told them, “I find no guilt in him.
ESV.39: But you have a custom that I should release one man for you at the Passover. So do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”
ESV.40: They cried out again, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.

NLT.1: After saying these things, Jesus crossed the Kidron Valley with his disciples and entered a grove of olive trees.
NLT.2: Judas, the betrayer, knew this place, because Jesus had often gone there with his disciples.
NLT.3: The leading priests and Pharisees had given Judas a contingent of Roman soldiers and Temple guards to accompany him. Now with blazing torches, lanterns, and weapons, they arrived at the olive grove.
NLT.4: Jesus fully realized all that was going to happen to him, so he stepped forward to meet them. “Who are you looking for?” he asked.
NLT.5: “Jesus the Nazarene,” they replied. “I AM he,” Jesus said. (Judas, who betrayed him, was standing with them.)
NLT.6: As Jesus said “I AM he,” they all drew back and fell to the ground!
NLT.7: Once more he asked them, “Who are you looking for?” And again they replied, “Jesus the Nazarene.”
NLT.8: “I told you that I AM he,” Jesus said. “And since I am the one you want, let these others go.”
NLT.9: He did this to fulfill his own statement: “I did not lose a single one of those you have given me.”
NLT.10: Then Simon Peter drew a sword and slashed off the right ear of Malchus, the high priest’s slave.
NLT.11: But Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword back into its sheath. Shall I not drink from the cup of suffering the Father has given me?”
NLT.12: So the soldiers, their commanding officer, and the Temple guards arrested Jesus and tied him up.
NLT.13: First they took him to Annas, since he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest at that time.
NLT.14: Caiaphas was the one who had told the other Jewish leaders, “It’s better that one man should die for the people.”
NLT.15: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another of the disciples. That other disciple was acquainted with the high priest, so he was allowed to enter the high priest’s courtyard with Jesus.
NLT.16: Peter had to stay outside the gate. Then the disciple who knew the high priest spoke to the woman watching at the gate, and she let Peter in.
NLT.17: The woman asked Peter, “You’re not one of that man’s disciples, are you?” “No,” he said, “I am not.”
NLT.18: Because it was cold, the household servants and the guards had made a charcoal fire. They stood around it, warming themselves, and Peter stood with them, warming himself.
NLT.19: Inside, the high priest began asking Jesus about his followers and what he had been teaching them.
NLT.20: Jesus replied, “Everyone knows what I teach. I have preached regularly in the synagogues and the Temple, where the people gather. I have not spoken in secret.
NLT.21: Why are you asking me this question? Ask those who heard me. They know what I said.”
NLT.22: Then one of the Temple guards standing nearby slapped Jesus across the face. “Is that the way to answer the high priest?” he demanded.
NLT.23: Jesus replied, “If I said anything wrong, you must prove it. But if I’m speaking the truth, why are you beating me?”
NLT.24: Then Annas bound Jesus and sent him to Caiaphas, the high priest.
NLT.25: Meanwhile, as Simon Peter was standing by the fire warming himself, they asked him again, “You’re not one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it, saying, “No, I am not.”
NLT.26: But one of the household slaves of the high priest, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Didn’t I see you out there in the olive grove with Jesus?”
NLT.27: Again Peter denied it. And immediately a rooster crowed.
NLT.28: Jesus’ trial before Caiaphas ended in the early hours of the morning. Then he was taken to the headquarters of the Roman governor. His accusers didn’t go inside because it would defile them, and they wouldn’t be allowed to celebrate the Passover.
NLT.29: So Pilate, the governor, went out to them and asked, “What is your charge against this man?”
NLT.30: “We wouldn’t have handed him over to you if he weren’t a criminal!” they retorted.
NLT.31: “Then take him away and judge him by your own law,” Pilate told them. “Only the Romans are permitted to execute someone,” the Jewish leaders replied.
NLT.32: (This fulfilled Jesus’ prediction about the way he would die. )
NLT.33: Then Pilate went back into his headquarters and called for Jesus to be brought to him. “Are you the king of the Jews?” he asked him.
NLT.34: Jesus replied, “Is this your own question, or did others tell you about me?”
NLT.35: “Am I a Jew?” Pilate retorted. “Your own people and their leading priests brought you to me for trial. Why? What have you done?”
NLT.36: Jesus answered, “My Kingdom is not an earthly kingdom. If it were, my followers would fight to keep me from being handed over to the Jewish leaders. But my Kingdom is not of this world.”
NLT.37: Pilate said, “So you are a king?” Jesus responded, “You say I am a king. Actually, I was born and came into the world to testify to the truth. All who love the truth recognize that what I say is true.”
NLT.38: “What is truth?” Pilate asked. Then he went out again to the people and told them, “He is not guilty of any crime.
NLT.39: But you have a custom of asking me to release one prisoner each year at Passover. Would you like me to release this ‘King of the Jews’?”
NLT.40: But they shouted back, “No! Not this man. We want Barabbas!” (Barabbas was a revolutionary.)

CSB17.37: “You are a king then?” Pilate asked.  “You say that I’m a king,” Jesus replied. “I was born for this, and I have come into the world <sup>ⓗ</sup>for this: to testify <sup>ⓘ</sup>to the truth. <sup>ⓙ</sup>Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”

AMP.1: Having said these things, Jesus left with His disciples and went across the ravine of the Kidron. There was a garden there, which He and His disciples entered.
AMP.2: Now Judas, who was betraying Him, knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with His disciples.
AMP.3: So Judas, having obtained the Roman cohort and some officers from the high priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
AMP.4: Then Jesus, knowing all that was about to happen to Him, went to them and asked, "Whom do you want?"
AMP.5: They answered Him, "Jesus the Nazarene." Jesus said, "I am He." And Judas, who was betraying Him, was also standing with them.
AMP.6: When Jesus said, "I am He," they drew back and fell to the ground.
AMP.7: Again He asked them, "Whom do you want?" And they said, "Jesus the Nazarene."
AMP.8: Jesus answered, "I told you that I am He; so if you want Me, let these men go on their way."
AMP.9: This was to fulfill and verify the words He had spoken, "Of those whom You have given Me, I have not lost even one."
AMP.10: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.
AMP.11: So Jesus said to Peter, "Put the sword [back] in its sheath! Shall I not drink the cup which My Father has given Me?"
AMP.12: So the cohort and their commander and the officers of the Jews arrested Jesus and bound Him,
AMP.13: and led Him to Annas first; for he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
AMP.14: It was Caiaphas who had advised the Jews that it was expedient for one man to die on behalf of the people.
AMP.15: Simon Peter and another disciple were following Jesus. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, so he went with Jesus into the courtyard of the [residence of the] high priest;
AMP.16: but Peter was standing outside at the door. So the other disciple (John), who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the doorkeeper, and brought Peter inside.
AMP.17: Then the servant girl who kept the door said to Peter, "You are not one of this Man's disciples, are you?" He said, "I am not."
AMP.18: Now the servants and the officers had made a fire of coals, because it was cold, and they were standing and warming themselves. And Peter was with them, standing and warming himself.
AMP.19: Then the high priest questioned Jesus about His disciples and about His teaching.
AMP.20: Jesus answered him, "I have spoken openly to the world. I always taught in a synagogue and in the temple [area], where all the Jews habitually congregate; and I said nothing in secret.
AMP.21: Why question Me? Question those who have heard what I said to them. They know what I said."
AMP.22: But when He said this, one of the officers who was standing nearby struck Jesus [in the face], saying, "Is that how You answer the high priest?"
AMP.23: Jesus replied, "If I have said anything wrong, make a formal statement about the wrong; but if [I spoke] properly, why did you strike Me?"
AMP.24: So Annas sent Him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
AMP.25: Now Simon Peter was [still] standing and warming himself. So they said to him, "You are not one of His disciples, are you?" He denied it and said, "I am not."
AMP.26: One of the high priest's servants, a relative of the one whose ear Peter cut off, said, "Did I not see you with Him in the garden?"
AMP.27: So Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
AMP.28: Then the Jews led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Praetorium (governor's palace). Now it was early and the Jews did not enter the Praetorium so that they would not be [ceremonially] unclean, but might [be able to] eat [and participate in the Feast of Unleavened Bread which began after] the Passover [supper].
AMP.29: So Pilate came out to them and asked, "What accusation do you bring against this Man?"
AMP.30: They answered, "If He were not a criminal, we would not have handed Him over to you [for judgment]."
AMP.31: Then Pilate said to them, "Take Him yourselves and judge Him according to your own law." The Jews said, "We are not permitted to put anyone to death."
AMP.32: This was to fulfill the word which Jesus had spoken to indicate by what manner of death He was going to die.
AMP.33: So Pilate went into the Praetorium again, and called Jesus and asked Him, "Are You the King of the Jews?"
AMP.34: Jesus replied, "Are you saying this on your own initiative, or did others tell you about Me?"
AMP.35: Pilate answered, "I am not a Jew, am I? Your own people and their chief priests have handed You over to me. What have You done [that is worthy of death]?"
AMP.36: Jesus replied, "My kingdom is not of this world [nor does it have its origin in this world]. If My kingdom were of this world, My servants would be fighting [hard] to keep Me from being handed over to the Jews; but as it is, My kingdom is not of this world."
AMP.37: So Pilate said to Him, "Then You are a King?" Jesus answered, "You say [correctly] that I am a King. This is why I was born, and for this I have come into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth [who is a friend of the truth and belongs to the truth] hears and listens carefully to My voice."
AMP.38:
Pilate said to Him [scornfully], "What is truth?"
And when he had said this, he went out to the Jews again and told them, "I find no guilt in Him [no crime, no cause for an accusation].
AMP.39: But you have a custom that I release someone for you at the Passover. So shall I release for you the King of the Jews?"
AMP.40: Then they all shouted back again, "Not this Man, but Barabbas!" Now Barabbas was a robber.

MSG.1: Jesus, having prayed this prayer, left with his disciples and crossed over the brook Kidron at a place where there was a garden. He and his disciples entered it.

MSG.5: He said, "That's me." The soldiers recoiled, totally taken aback. Judas, his betrayer, stood out like a sore thumb.
MSG.7: Jesus asked again, "Who are you after?" They answered, "Jesus the Nazarene."

MSG.11: Jesus ordered Peter, "Put back your sword. Do you think for a minute I'm not going to drink this cup the Father gave me?"
MSG.12: Then the Roman soldiers under their commander, joined by the Jewish police, seized Jesus and tied him up.
MSG.13: They took him first to Annas, father-in-law of Caiaphas. Caiaphas was the Chief Priest that year.
MSG.14: It was Caiaphas who had advised the Jews that it was to their advantage that one man die for the people.
MSG.15: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. That other disciple was known to the Chief Priest, and so he went in with Jesus to the Chief Priest's courtyard.
MSG.16: Peter had to stay outside. Then the other disciple went out, spoke to the doorkeeper, and got Peter in.
MSG.17: The young woman who was the doorkeeper said to Peter, "Aren't you one of this man's disciples?" He said, "No, I'm not."
MSG.18: The servants and police had made a fire because of the cold and were huddled there warming themselves. Peter stood with them, trying to get warm.
MSG.19: Annas interrogated Jesus regarding his disciples and his teaching.
MSG.20: Jesus answered, "I've spoken openly in public. I've taught regularly in meeting places and the Temple, where the Jews all come together. Everything has been out in the open. I've said nothing in secret.
MSG.21: So why are you treating me like a conspirator? Question those who have been listening to me. They know well what I have said. My teachings have all been aboveboard."
MSG.22: When he said this, one of the policemen standing there slapped Jesus across the face, saying, "How dare you speak to the Chief Priest like that!"
MSG.23: Jesus replied, "If I've said something wrong, prove it. But if I've spoken the plain truth, why this slapping around?"
MSG.24: Then Annas sent him, still tied up, to the Chief Priest Caiaphas.
MSG.25: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was back at the fire, still trying to get warm. The others there said to him, "Aren't you one of his disciples?" He denied it, "Not me."
MSG.26: One of the Chief Priest's servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, said, "Didn't I see you in the garden with him?"
MSG.27: Again, Peter denied it. Just then a rooster crowed.
MSG.28: They led Jesus then from Caiaphas to the Roman governor's palace. It was early morning. They themselves didn't enter the palace because they didn't want to be disqualified from eating the Passover.
MSG.29: So Pilate came out to them and spoke. "What charge do you bring against this man?"
MSG.30: They said, "If he hadn't been doing something evil, do you think we'd be here bothering you?"
MSG.31: Pilate said, "You take him. Judge him by your law." The Jews said, "We're not allowed to kill anyone."
MSG.32: (This would confirm Jesus' word indicating the way he would die.)
MSG.33: Pilate went back into the palace and called for Jesus. He said, "Are you the 'King of the Jews'?"
MSG.34: Jesus answered, "Are you saying this on your own, or did others tell you this about me?"
MSG.35: Pilate said, "Do I look like a Jew? Your people and your high priests turned you over to me. What did you do?"
MSG.36: "My kingdom," said Jesus, "doesn't consist of what you see around you. If it did, my followers would fight so that I wouldn't be handed over to the Jews. But I'm not that kind of king, not the world's kind of king."
MSG.37: Then Pilate said, "So, are you a king or not?" Jesus answered, "You tell me. Because I am King, I was born and entered the world so that I could witness to the truth. Everyone who cares for truth, who has any feeling for the truth, recognizes my voice."
MSG.38: Pilate said, "What is truth?" Then he went back out to the Jews and told them, "I find nothing wrong in this man.
MSG.39: It's your custom that I pardon one prisoner at Passover. Do you want me to pardon the 'King of the Jews'?"
MSG.40: They shouted back, "Not this one, but Barabbas!" Barabbas was a Jewish freedom fighter.

NIV.1:
Jesus Arrested
When he had finished praying, Jesus left with his disciples and crossed the Kidron Valley. On the other side there was an olive grove, and he and his disciples went into it.
NIV.2: Now Judas, who betrayed him, knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with his disciples.
NIV.3: So Judas came to the grove, guiding a detachment of soldiers and some officials from the chief priests and Pharisees. They were carrying torches, lanterns and weapons.
NIV.4: Jesus, knowing all that was going to happen to him, went out and asked them, “Who is it you want?”
NIV.5:
“Jesus of Nazareth,” they replied.
“I am he,” Jesus said. (And Judas the traitor was standing there with them.)
NIV.6: When Jesus said, “I am he,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
NIV.7:
Again he asked them, “Who is it you want?”
And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
NIV.8: “I told you that I am he,” Jesus answered. “If you are looking for me, then let these men go.”
NIV.9: This happened so that the words he had spoken would be fulfilled: “I have not lost one of those you gave me.”
NIV.10: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priest’s servant, cutting off his right ear. (The servant’s name was Malchus.)
NIV.11: Jesus commanded Peter, “Put your sword away! Shall I not drink the cup the Father has given me?”
NIV.12:
Jesus Taken to Annas
Then the detachment of soldiers with its commander and the Jewish officials arrested Jesus. They bound him
NIV.13: and brought him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
NIV.14: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it would be good if one man died for the people.
NIV.15:
Peter’s First Denial
Simon Peter and another disciple were following Jesus. Because this disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priest’s courtyard,
NIV.16: but Peter had to wait outside at the door. The other disciple, who was known to the high priest, came back, spoke to the girl on duty there and brought Peter in.
NIV.17:
“You are not one of his disciples, are you?” the girl at the door asked Peter.
He replied, “I am not.”
NIV.18: It was cold, and the servants and officials stood around a fire they had made to keep warm. Peter also was standing with them, warming himself.
NIV.19:
The High Priest Questions Jesus
Meanwhile, the high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
NIV.20: “I have spoken openly to the world,” Jesus replied. “I always taught in synagogues or at the temple, where all the Jews come together. I said nothing in secret.
NIV.21: Why question me? Ask those who heard me. Surely they know what I said.”
NIV.22: When Jesus said this, one of the officials nearby struck him in the face. “Is this the way you answer the high priest?” he demanded.
NIV.23: “If I said something wrong,” Jesus replied, “testify as to what is wrong. But if I spoke the truth, why did you strike me?”
NIV.24: Then Annas sent him, still bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
NIV.25:
Peter’s Second and Third Denials
As Simon Peter stood warming himself, he was asked, “You are not one of his disciples, are you?”
He denied it, saying, “I am not.”
NIV.26: One of the high priest’s servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, challenged him, “Didn’t I see you with him in the olive grove?”
NIV.27: Again Peter denied it, and at that moment a rooster began to crow.
NIV.28:
Jesus Before Pilate
Then the Jews led Jesus from Caiaphas to the palace of the Roman governor. By now it was early morning, and to avoid ceremonial uncleanness the Jews did not enter the palace; they wanted to be able to eat the Passover.
NIV.29: So Pilate came out to them and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
NIV.30: “If he were not a criminal,” they replied, “we would not have handed him over to you.”
NIV.31:
Pilate said, “Take him yourselves and judge him by your own law.”
“But we have no right to execute anyone,” the Jews objected.
NIV.32: This happened so that the words Jesus had spoken indicating the kind of death he was going to die would be fulfilled.
NIV.33: Pilate then went back inside the palace, summoned Jesus and asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
NIV.34: “Is that your own idea,” Jesus asked, “or did others talk to you about me?”
NIV.35: “Am I a Jew?” Pilate replied. “It was your people and your chief priests who handed you over to me. What is it you have done?”
NIV.36: Jesus said, “My kingdom is not of this world. If it were, my servants would fight to prevent my arrest by the Jews. But now my kingdom is from another place.”
NIV.37:
“You are a king, then!” said Pilate.
Jesus answered, “You are right in saying I am a king. In fact, for this reason I was born, and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone on the side of truth listens to me.”
NIV.38: “What is truth?” Pilate asked. With this he went out again to the Jews and said, “I find no basis for a charge against him.
NIV.39: But it is your custom for me to release to you one prisoner at the time of the Passover. Do you want me to release ‘the king of the Jews’?”
NIV.40: They shouted back, “No, not him! Give us Barabbas!” Now Barabbas had taken part in a rebellion.
